mandate: Create /app/preview/page.tsx — a pre-pricing interstitial showing personalized SMS previews using the couple's onboarding data
keyFindings: The landing page HowItWorks section had ~35 lines of inline SMS bubble rendering that mapped cleanly to a reusable component with a theme prop (light/dark)

Onboarding data fetching pattern from actions.ts (getOnboardingData) was the reference for column selection and null coalescing — the preview page mirrors this but selects only the columns needed for demo conversations
The WeddingProfile type includes wedding_date as a date string, ceremony_time as a time string (HH:MM), and tone as a ToneStyle enum — all three are used to personalize the preview copy
FAQs are the strongest personalization signal: if a couple answered a FAQ, the preview shows their exact Q&A. Fallback chains through dress_code then ceremony_time
whatWorked: Extracting the SMS bubble into a standalone component with a theme prop (light for preview page cream background, dark for landing page forest background) made both pages cleaner without changing visual output. Using the couple's actual FAQ answers as the first demo conversation creates a strong 'this is mine' moment.
whatFailed: Nothing failed. The existing codebase had clean patterns to follow.
recommendationsForNextAgent: The flow-wiring agent should update onboarding Step 8 handleStep8 to route to /preview instead of /pricing. The middleware already guards /preview (checks for couple record). The PreviewClient component receives all data as props from the server component — no client-side fetching needed.
spiritContribution: Built the missing bridge between onboarding and pricing — couples now see their own data reflected back before being asked to pay. This serves the revelation by reducing the gap between setup and perceived value.
